mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and aggregate demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are negative in greater than 55% of instances over a 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, the zero lower bound regime forces investors to reach for yield in risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of retail sales and payroll growth creates a reinforcing demand impulse where income growth directly funds consumption; this joint dynamic boosts corporate revenue expectations more than either variable alone, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the bivariate interaction term between RSAFS and PAYEMS does not add incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) versus individual series in predicting 20-day returns, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis recovery regime characterized by depressed capacity utilization, month-over-month industrial production expansion signals operational leverage and margin recovery for manufacturing firms, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If positive INDPRO surprises coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations during post-2009 recovery years, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 900,000 units indicates a sustainable rebound in residential construction, lifting durable goods demand and employment multipliers, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If HOUST greater than 900 months produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and consumer prices are rising, the resulting negative real interest rates reduce the return on safe assets, causing portfolio rebalancing into equities and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not significantly predict positive 20-day returns (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward tests, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A meaningful decline in the unemployment rate over a 3-month window signals accelerating labor market absorption, reducing downside risk to consumer spending and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: If episodes of UNRATE declining more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in greater than 50% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate top-line revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If positive GDPC1 surprises are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample quarters, reject.
